Noel Review - Empires & Puzzles - Joyful Sledge Kid Strategy Guide

Meet Noel, the festive 5-star Dark hero from the Winter family who brings joy and frustration to the battlefield. With Fast mana speed, he boosts ally health and provides a sticky Dodge buff that grants a Minion upon removal. In this review, we analyze his unique mechanics, compare his stats to the 2026 meta, and decide if he is worth your precious mats.
Noel, The Joyful Sledge Kid: Dodge, Heal, and Minions on Ice

Noel is a 5-star Legendary Dark hero introduced during the Winter Event in December 2025. As a modern Seasonal hero, he brings a festive twist to the classic support role. He is a Fast speed healer who doesn't just patch up wounds but also proactively protects his team with a unique "sticky" Dodge mechanic. While most healers sit back and wait for damage, Noel slides into action quickly to prevent it. He essentially asks the enemy, "Why hit my team when you could hit nothing instead?"

Noel's artwork is pure, unadulterated holiday cheer, perhaps bordering on manic. He features a living snowman character joyfully hurtling down a slope on a wooden sledge. The vibrant purple and blue color palette fits his Dark element while maintaining the icy Winter theme. It is a lighthearted design that contrasts sharply with the grim, battle-hardened warriors usually found in the Dark roster. He looks like he is having the time of his life, completely oblivious to the violence of the raid arena.

With a base Power of 1259, Noel sits comfortably within the modern stat tier for heroes released in late 2025. His stat distribution is well-balanced but leans slightly towards defense and health, which is ideal for his role as a support hero.
Respectable, ensuring his tile damage isn't negligible, but not his primary focus.
Solid durability allows him to survive initial snipes.
A massive health pool that synergizes perfectly with his Monk class and defensive capabilities.
Compared to older healers, his raw stats act as a significant buffer, allowing him to survive the high-damage output of contemporary attackers. He is built to last.
Sledge Bash is a multi-layered defensive skill packed into a Fast mana speed chassis.

The 760 boosted health is a flat amount. While percentage-based heals scale better with the massive HP pools of modern troops, 760 at Fast speed is highly efficient. It acts as immediate mitigation, erasing the damage of a slash attack or a light special skill.
This is the crown jewel of his kit. Unlike traditional dodge which lasts for a set number of turns, this dodge lasts
until the ally successfully dodges once
. It is "sticky." This makes it incredibly annoying for snipers, as the buff won't just expire if you wait it out. You
to engage with it or dispel it.

If the dodge is triggered (or dispelled!), the ally receives a Snowsprite Minion with 20% HP and 20% Attack. This is brilliant design. If you miss the hero (dodge), they are safe. If you trigger the dodge, they get a meat shield (minion). It creates a "damned if you do, damned if you don't" scenario for the attacker.

. With a level 29 mana troop or a bard bonus, he can fire in 6 tiles (Ghost) or 7 tiles regularly. This speed is critical for getting the dodge up before the enemy fires.

The Frost passive deals 484 damage over 4 turns per minion hit. If 3 minions hit an enemy, that is significant ticking damage.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Noel benefits from the updated Winter family and a synergistic passive.

The Winter Family bonus has been updated to provide small boosts to Minion stats (Health and Attack). While not game-breaking on its own, it incentivizes pairing Noel with other Winter heroes (like the updated Krampus or Mother North) to make those Snowsprites slightly beefier.

This passive adds offensive pressure to his defensive kit. Every time a minion hits an enemy, they receive a Frost DOT (Damage Over Time). It scales with the hero's power. It ensures that even if Noel is just healing, he is contributing to whittling down the enemy team.

A standard defensive Aether Power. It reduces all incoming damage by a percentage for the first few turns of the battle. This helps ensure Noel survives the opening volley in Rush wars or against fast aggressive defenses, guaranteeing he gets his first cast off.
The Winter event has given us titans of the meta like Mother North and Krampus. Noel enters the fray not as a reviver or a taunter, but as a proactive mitigator.
In the era of 2026, raw healing numbers often struggle to keep up with damage that can exceed 2000 in a single hit. Noel sidesteps this issue by relying on

. His Fast speed allows him to keep pace with the current speed meta. The sticky nature of his dodge makes him unique compared to heroes like Kadilen (Costume) whose dodge can be waited out. He is excellent in both offense and defense, though perhaps slightly better on offense where you can time his dodge against specific enemy threats.
Current review focuses on the base hero as no costume was specified or released for Noel at this time.

If you pulled Noel, rejoice! A 5-star healer is the backbone of any roster. Prioritize leveling him. His heal and dodge will carry you through difficult map stages and allow you to punch above your weight in raids.

Noel is a tactical tool. He isn't a "place and forget" defense hero like some tanks, but on offense, he offers control. The "sticky" dodge is his selling point. If you lack a reliable dodge hero, he is worth the Alpha Aethers. If you already have fully maxed Constance or similar fast supports, compare their utility. Noel's advantage is the Boosted Health combined with the Dodge.
The community reaction to Noel has been largely positive, though some beta testers felt he was "too strong" initially.
One player noted: "The 760 health boost is fast... but 760 is just not a lot compared to modern HP stats."
Another countered: "It's a guaranteed buff that triggers eventually... consider it a 2500 heal if you dodge a sniper."
The general consensus is that he is "fun" and "annoying to face," fitting the mischievous winter theme perfectly.

Noel is a fantastic addition to the Dark roster. He combines the best aspects of a healer and a dodge-buffer into a Fast mana package. While his raw healing numbers might look static on paper, the utility of negating damage entirely via dodge is mathematically infinite. He brings joy to his allies and coal to his enemies.
A solid A-tier hero who will find a home in many war teams and raid offenses.
A detailed review of Noel, the Fast mana purple healer from the Winter family. We analyze his unique sticky Dodge mechanics, health boost, and how he stacks up in the current meta.
